root_cause.text: The WebSocket frame parsing logic failed to validate that the most significant bit of a 64-bit payload length was zero, potentially allowing negative payload lengths to be parsed and processed.
root_cause.evidence: The patch in `WsFrameBase.java` adds a check `if (payloadLength < 0)` after parsing an 8-byte payload length, along with a comment stating that RFC 6455 requires the most significant bit to be zero and throwing a protocol error if it is violated.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The WebSocket frame parsing logic failed to validate that the most significant bit of a 64-bit payload length was zero, potentially allowing negative payload lengths to be parsed and processed.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch in `WsFrameBase.java` adds a check `if (payloadLength < 0)` after parsing an 8-byte payload length, along with a comment stating that RFC 6455 requires the most significant bit to be zero and throwing a protocol error if it is violated.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add an error message property for 64-bit WebSocket payload MSB validation failures.
intent_record.what: Added a new error message property for an invalid WebSocket frame payload.
intent_record.how: Provides the text for an error thrown when the most significant bit of a 64-bit payload is incorrectly set.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add boundary validation to reject WebSocket frames with negative extended payload lengths.
intent_record.what: Adds a check to ensure the parsed 8-byte payload length is not negative.
intent_record.how: Throws a WsIOException with a protocol error if the payload length is negative, enforcing the RFC 6455 requirement that the MSB must be zero.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update changelog to document WebSocket payload length validation fix for bug 64563.
intent_record.what: Added a new subsection to the XML changelog documenting a fix for WebSocket payload length validation.
intent_record.how: Updates the project documentation to make users aware of the fix for bug 64563.

grouping_result.intent: Update changelog to document WebSocket payload length validation fix for bug 64563.
Add boundary validation to reject WebSocket frames with negative extended payload lengths.

consistency_review.core_intent: Add an error message and corresponding changelog entry for a WebSocket payload length validation fix.

relevance_review.reason: The group adds an error message and changelog entry directly related to the fix for the WebSocket payload length validation vulnerability.
The group directly addresses the vulnerability root cause by adding validation to reject WebSocket frames with negative payload lengths, which directly fixes the missing most significant bit check.

candidates.rationale: This line calculates the 64-bit payload length without subsequently validating that the most significant bit is zero, allowing negative payload lengths.
This initiates the block to read a 64-bit payload length where the missing validation should be added.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement assigns the 64-bit payload length without subsequently validating that the most significant bit is zero (i.e., that it is not negative), leading to the vulnerability.
